heroFact: Alcohol leaves your bloodstream at a fairly constant rate — roughly one standard drink per hour — regardless of coffee, cold showers, or exercise.

timeline.label: Alcohol begins entering the bloodstream from the stomach and small intestine
The liver metabolizes roughly one standard drink per hour
timeline.note: Body weight, food intake, and biological sex all affect the exact rate.

timeline.label: Most alcohol has cleared the bloodstream
timeline.note: A hangover and lingering effects can still continue after alcohol itself is gone.
